media_path: 02 Building Environments - The Basics/02 What are PBR Materials.mp4

items.text: PBR stands for Physically Based Rendering, a standard approach to materials.
items.speaker: instructor

items.text: PBR materials have three core properties: Base Color, Roughness, and Metalness.
items.speaker: instructor

items.text: Base Color defines the albedo of the surface without lighting.
items.speaker: instructor

items.text: Roughness controls how smooth or rough a surface appears, affecting specular reflections.
items.speaker: instructor

items.text: Metalness determines if a surface behaves like a metal or a dielectric.
items.speaker: instructor

items.text: Metals have colored specular reflections and no diffuse. Dielectrics have white specular.
items.speaker: instructor

items.text: UE5 uses the Microfacet BRDF model for realistic surface rendering.
items.speaker: instructor

items.text: Example: A rusty metal pipe has high roughness but full metalness for a weathered look.
items.speaker: instructor

items.text: In Unreal, you create materials in the Material Editor using node-based graph.
items.speaker: instructor

claims.statement: PBR materials use Base Color, Roughness, and Metalness as core properties.
Metals and dielectrics have different reflection behavior in PBR rendering.

gaps.description: System missed specific numerical values for roughness/metalness parameter ranges
